display.purpose: Page title and result-state post title only. One per surface.

body.purpose: Primary reading text and form copy. 1rem floor.

label.purpose: Field group headers (Also include, Published between) and section markers. Uppercase + tracking is the signal.

mono.purpose: Filenames, paths, tag chips, URLs, code-shaped content. The Two-Voices Rule signal.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (hover, focus, open, close).

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Color and opacity transitions on interactive elements.

motion.name: duration-medium
motion.purpose: Disclosure reveals and panel state transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The CTA. Pill-shaped, full-width on key actions like Fetch from Patreon and Use for filename.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; height: 3rem; padding: 0 1rem; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 1.625rem; font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--primary); filter: brightness(1.05); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 70%); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action. Hairline border on the page background, fills with muted on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0 1rem; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 1.625rem; font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--muted); color: var(--foreground); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 70%); }

components.name: Input with leading icon
components.description: URL field. 48px tall (touch target), mono font (URLs are machine strings), lucide icon in the left padding.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { position: relative; width: 100%; } .ds-input-icon { position: absolute; left: 0.875rem; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%); width: 16px; height: 16px; color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--muted-foreground), transparent 30%); pointer-events: none; } .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 3rem; padding: 0 0.75rem 0 2.5rem; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; outline: none; transition: box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--muted-foreground), transparent 45%); } .ds-input:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 55%); }

components.name: Mono Tag Chip
components.description: Read-only chip for tags returned from the backend. Mono font is the signal that this is machine-produced data.
components.css: .ds-chip-mono { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.25rem 0.5rem; background: var(--muted); color: var(--muted-foreground); border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; line-height: 1; }

components.name: Filter Chip (active)
components.description: Interactive content-type filter (Audio, Drive links, Video, etc.). Active state uses accent surface with brighter accent-foreground text.
components.css: .ds-chip-filter { padding: 0.375rem 0.75rem; background: var(--background); color: var(--muted-foreground); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-chip-filter:hover { color: var(--foreground); border-color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--muted-foreground), transparent 70%); } .ds-chip-filter:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 50%); } .ds-chip-filter--active { background: var(--accent); color: var(--accent-foreground); border-color: var(--accent); } .ds-chip-filter--active:hover { color: var(--accent-foreground); }

components.name: Mode Toggle Row
components.description: Card-row pattern for binary modes (Don't download audio, Preview only). Full-row tap target with internal checkbox + label + hint.
components.css: .ds-mode-toggle { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 0.75rem; padding: 0.75rem; background: var(--background); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 0.5rem; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-mode-toggle:hover { border-color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--muted-foreground), transparent 70%); } .ds-mode-toggle__check { margin-top: 0.125rem; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--primary); width: 1rem; height: 1rem; } .ds-mode-toggle__body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.25rem; min-width: 0; } .ds-mode-toggle__label { font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--foreground); } .ds-mode-toggle__hint { font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; color: var(--muted-foreground); line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Result Row
components.description: Multi-post list row. The entire title-area is the activation button (tap row to apply). External-link disclosure renders as a sibling so anchors stay reachable.
components.css: .ds-result-row { background: var(--background); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 0.625rem; overflow: hidden; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-result-row:hover { border-color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--muted-foreground), transparent 60%); } .ds-result-row__button { width: 100%; padding: 1rem; background: transparent; color: inherit; border: 0; text-align: left; cursor: pointer; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.625rem; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-result-row__button:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--accent), transparent 60%); } .ds-result-row__button:focus-visible { outline: none; background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--accent), transparent 60%); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 60%); } .ds-result-row__head { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 0.625rem; min-width: 0; } .ds-result-row__id { flex-shrink: 0; padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 0.375rem; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.625rem; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-result-row__title { font-family: 'Bricolage Grotesque Variable', 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--foreground); line-height: 1.4; flex: 1; min-width: 0; word-break: break-word; } .ds-result-row__meta { display: flex; align-items: center; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.75rem; font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; } .ds-result-row__artist { color: var(--muted-foreground); }

narrative.overview: Tailscale's admin dashboard meets Raycast's empty states, with a little of Cron / Notion Calendar's quiet density. The chrome is utility-shaped (minimal, predictable, clean) but the seams carry the personality: long-task narration during patreon-dl fetches, empty-state copy, status feedback, the dictionary load-error retry banner. The visual system explicitly rejects four reflexes: not Spotify (saturated single accent on near-black), not Audacity (cold utilitarian DAW chrome), not Linear / Notion / generic productivity SaaS (and especially not the soulless-modern-app trap of minimal-without-personality), and not NSFW aggregator visual codes (aggressive red/orange/black, dense thumbnail grids, hard contrast, saturated hover states). Light and dark are first-class equals; the same character lives in both. Built for desktop including 21:9, 32:9, 5K, and 8K ultrawides.
keyCharacteristics: Cool slate surfaces (hue 265, low chroma) carry the whole room.
One teal-cyan accent (hue 195, deeper L for confidence) is the only chromatic interest.
Foreground is warm cream (hue 85), the only warm element against the cool room.
Light and dark are first-class equals; the same character lives in both.
Flat by default. No shadows at rest. Lift comes from value.
Built for desktop including ultrawide (21:9, 32:9, 5K, 8K) as first-class viewports.
Designed for dim desks and imperfect eyesight: generous type, 44px+ tap targets.
Two voices: display sans for human writing, mono for machine writing.

rules.name: The Cool-Surfaces Rule
rules.body: Every surface sits at hue 265 with chroma between 0.015 and 0.040. Surfaces never read warm; warmth is reserved for the foreground and primary roles.

rules.name: The One-Accent Rule
rules.body: Teal-cyan (hue 195) is the only chromatic interest in the chrome. It carries the CTA, the focus ring, and the active filter state. No second accent competes for attention.

rules.name: The Warm Foreground Rule
rules.body: Text and primary foreground elements tilt warm-cream (hue 85). This single warm element against the cool room is the system's warm-cool axis. Pure white text is forbidden.

rules.name: The No-Cold-Accent Rule
rules.body: Despite cool slate surfaces, no Linear-blue or crypto-neon ever appears. The accent earns its place by being a specific brushed-metal teal-cyan (hue 195), not a generic productivity-tool blue.

rules.name: The Two-Voices Rule
rules.body: Display and body sans are for what the human writes. Mono is for what the machine writes. A label in mono or a filename in display is a bug, not a style choice.

rules.name: The 1rem-Floor Rule
rules.body: Body text is 1rem (16px) or larger. Always. Chips and captions may drop to 0.8125rem, but body copy never does.

rules.name: The Display-Sparingly Rule
rules.body: Bricolage Grotesque appears only in the page title and the headline of a result-state surface. Display type used everywhere is display type used nowhere.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: At rest, no surface in the system casts a shadow. Lift only happens in response to state.

rules.name: The Value-Lift Rule
rules.body: When a surface needs to feel elevated, the lift is communicated by raising the background lightness within the same hue family, not by adding a shadow.

rules.name: The Tinted-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Where shadows do appear (popover, dialog), they tint toward the cool slate family, never pure black.

rules.name: The Personality-In-Seams Rule
rules.body: Long-running fetches narrate progress (not a single static spinner). Empty states teach the interface in librarian-voice. Errors are plain-English and offer recovery inline. Character earns its place where it tells the user something they didn't already know, never decoratively.

dos: Do treat the room as cool and the foreground as warm. Surfaces sit at hue 265 with low chroma; foreground text and the primary accent provide the warmth.
Do use Amplifier Cyan only where attention is intentional: the CTA, the focus ring, the active filter chip.
Do use JetBrains Mono for every filename, tag chip, raw path, URL, and machine-generated string.
Do floor body text at 1rem and touch targets at 44 by 44px (48 by 48px on the primary CTA which is 3rem tall).
Do put the personality in the seams: long-task narration, empty-state copy, status feedback, the dictionary load-error retry banner.
Do scale into available room on ultrawide. At 2xl+, cap top-trio column widths and let dense surfaces (FileBrowser) use the full container.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion: any motion not essential to feedback is stripped behind motion-safe:.
Do verify both light and dark mode at WCAG AA before merging. Both are first-class equals.
donts: Don't look like Spotify or any commercial music app. The Amplifier Cyan must never read as neon.
Don't look like Audacity or any pro-audio DAW. No cold-grey utilitarian chrome.

Don't look like Linear, Notion, or generic productivity SaaS, and especially don't fall into the soulless-modern-app trap of minimal-without-personality. The accent is teal-cyan at hue 195, not a cold blue at 250.
Don't borrow visual codes from NSFW aggregators: aggressive red/orange/black palettes, dense thumbnail grids, hard contrast, saturated hover states.
Don't leak backend jargon into UI vocabulary (itag, CDP, signed URL, Playwright, metadata_only, dry_run).
Don't introduce a second saturated accent color. The system is Committed to one warm-cool tension.
Don't go warm-rose, mauve, or sepia. Past iterations failed in those directions.
Don't hard-cap the layout at SaaS-default 1280-1536px. The 2xl cap is 3200px; FileBrowser uses the full container.
Don't treat dark as the canonical mode and light as the polite afterthought. Both are first-class.
Don't use orchestrated mount animations or staggered section reveals. Motion is Responsive, not Choreographed.
Don't use side-stripe borders, gradient text, glassmorphism, hero-metric SaaS templates, identical card grids, or modals as a first thought.
Don't use em dashes in user-facing UI copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses.
Don't use #000 or #fff anywhere. Every neutral tilts toward the brand hue family.
